title: Guard against N+1 in Eloquent/Doctrine relations
description: Eager-load related models so a list view issues a bounded number of queries, not one per row.
body: Use when iterating models that access a relation inside the loop. Do not access a lazy relation per row in a list. Actions: eager-load with Eloquent's with()/load() (or Doctrine fetch joins / fetch=EAGER where justified), select only needed columns, and consider chunking large result sets. Validation signals include a query-count assertion in a test (Laravel's assertQueryCount / DB query log) showing a constant number of queries regardless of row count. Risk boundary: an unbounded N+1 degrades linearly with data and can take down a database under load; verify the query count, not just that the page renders.
